explainability.findings: A privacy policy outlines individuals' rights regarding automated decision-making, specifically including the right to explanations of the logic involved.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document for ITW Graphics provides evidence for the pillars of explainability, governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency. It supports these pillars by outlining individuals' rights regarding automated decision-making, including the right to explanations of the logic involved and the option for human review. Furthermore, the policy details rights related to profiling, data collection opt-outs, and the limitation of sensitive data use, demonstrating mechanisms for governance, privacy, and transparency in AI systems.
relevant_sources.title: ITW Graphics Privacy Policy

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A privacy policy details rights related to profiling, data collection opt-outs, and the limitation of sensitive data use. Furthermore, a report highlights shareholder proposals focused on improving AI development and suggests a focus on establishing policies and mechanisms for managing AI risks.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The "Portfolios on the Ballot 2024" report provides evidence for the **governance** and **oversight** pillars of responsible AI. The report highlights shareholder proposals at major companies focused on improving AI oversight and development, indicating a material interest from investors in these areas. This suggests a focus on establishing policies and mechanisms for managing AI risks.
relevant_sources.title: Portfolios on the Ballot 2024

oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

privacy.findings: A privacy policy details individuals' rights related to profiling and data collection opt-outs. The policy also specifies the limitation of sensitive data use.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

transparency.findings: A privacy policy outlines individuals' rights regarding automated decision-making, including the right to explanations of the logic involved, the option for human review, and rights related to profiling, data collection opt-outs, and the limitation of sensitive data use. Additionally, a project summary details the use of machine learning algorithms for real-time weld quality diagnosis, decision, and control, and mentions the system's ability to learn and improve as it operates.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document for ITW Graphics provides evidence for the pillars of explainability, governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency. It supports these pillars by outlining individuals' rights regarding automated decision-making, including the right to explanations of the logic involved and the option for human review. Furthermore, the policy details rights related to profiling, data collection opt-outs, and the limitation of sensitive data use, demonstrating mechanisms for governance, privacy, and transparency in AI systems.

relevant_sources.summary: This project summary provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It details the use of machine learning algorithms for real-time weld quality diagnosis, decision, and control, and explicitly mentions the system's ability to learn and improve as it operates, indicating transparency about the AI's function and application.
relevant_sources.title: Improving Welding in Real Time with AI

summary.overall_findings: Based on 9 publicly available sources, Illinois Tool Works' published materials address 5 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars at the policy level. A privacy policy outlines individuals' rights regarding automated decision-making, including the right to explanations of the logic involved and the option for human review. This policy also details rights related to profiling and data collection opt-outs. Furthermore, a report highlights shareholder proposals focused on improving AI oversight. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness or external accountability.
